agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, Operations demands no location sharing. Both claim their requirement is non-negotiable. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Order Fulfillment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person physically transporting a package from origin to customer."},{"term":"Real-time Location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The time period during which a courier is actively en route to the customer with their order."},{"term":"Location Granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is shared — ranges from exact GPS coordinates to approximate zone (e.g., 'nearby', street-level, neighborhood)."},{"term":"Active Delivery","definition":"The state of an order after pickup and before confirmed drop-off, during which tracking may be relevant."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) — exact GPS makes customer anxious or reveals personal stops","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core contradiction"},{"scenario":"Courier is near customer's home but delivering another order first (multi-stop route) — exact GPS creates confusion and complaints","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core contradiction"},{"scenario":"Courier's GPS signal is lost or inaccurate — stale exact coordinates mislead the customer","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without resolving the core contradiction"},{"scenario":"Courier is off-shift or between deliveries — location must never leak outside the active delivery window regardless of policy chosen","expected_behavior":"This is a privacy invariant: location data of any granularity must only be accessible during active delivery state. This holds under any resolution."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or records courier GPS path — creates safety/stalking risk for courier","expected_behavior":"A real physical safety concern that weighs into the resolution. Exact coordinates create liability exposure."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High impact either way. Customer Success argues transparency drives conversion and retention (revenue). Operations argues driver churn is increasing cost-to-serve (recruitment, training, onboarding costs per driver lost). Both claims likely have merit. Without data on relative magnitudes, neither can be dismissed.","operational":"Driver retention directly affects delivery capacity and SLA compliance. If drivers leave due to surveillance concerns, operational capacity degrades regardless of how good the customer-facing feature is. Conversely, lack of tracking increases WISMO (Where Is My Order) call volume and support costs.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability — but 'exact GPS coordinates at all times' may not be what they actually need. The real customer job-to-be-done is likely 'know when my delivery will arrive' not 'surveil my courier.' Drivers are also users of the platform and their experience directly determines service quality.","risk":"CRITICAL: Exact GPS sharing creates legal liability under GDPR, CCPA, and labor privacy regulations in many jurisdictions. Courier location data is personal data. Sharing it without proper legal basis and courier consent is a compliance risk. Additionally, exact coordinates create physical safety risks (stalking, confrontation). This is not just a preference — it is a liability question.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want delivery predictability. Exact GPS is one solution but not the only one. ETA countdown, proximity indicators, or stage-based tracking may satisfy the actual need."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance perception. Driver retention is existential for a courier business — no drivers means no deliveries. Their consent and comfort with location sharing is both an ethical and operational concern."},{"group":"Customer Success Team","impact":"Measured on customer satisfaction and retention. Needs a tracking feature that reduces WISMO contacts and increases customer confidence."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Measured on driver retention and delivery capacity. Needs a solution that does not exacerbate driver churn."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but MUST be. Exact GPS sharing of employee/contractor location to third parties has regulatory implications."},{"group":"Executive Leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict. This is a strategic product decision about marketplace balance (customer vs. driver experience) that engineering cannot and should not resolve."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Confidence is 40 because the specification contains a direct, unresolvable contradiction between two authoritative stakeholder teams. Both requirements as stated cannot coexist — one says 'must show exact GPS' and the other says 'must never show location.' No amount of analysis can produce valid acceptance criteria when the fundamental business policy is in dispute. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not weighed in, which is essential for any location-sharing feature involving workers. The spec quality is fundamentally broken.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine contradiction that cannot be resolved by engineering","Driver location during non-delivery periods must never be exposed (privacy invariant regardless of outcome)","Legal/compliance review is mandatory before any location-sharing feature ships","The customer's real job-to-be-done is delivery predictability, not courier surveillance"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder team's requirement should take priority","Whether a middle-ground solution (approximate location, ETA-only) would satisfy both teams","The actual financial impact of driver churn vs. customer churn","Regulatory requirements in the specific jurisdictions this platform operates in"],"assumptions":["Both teams have legitimate authority to set requirements in their domain","No executive decision has already been made that supersedes either team's position","Couriers are contractors or employees with privacy rights regarding location data","The platform operates in jurisdictions with personal data protection regulations"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location never be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from two teams with legitimate authority. This is a strategic business decision about marketplace balance (customer experience vs. driver experience) that must be resolved by executive leadership, not by engineering or domain analysis.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success","Operations","Legal/Compliance","Executive Leadership"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS as Customer Success demands — accept driver churn risk and legal exposure","B: Show no location as Operations demands — accept higher WISMO volume and potential customer churn","C: Compromise with approximate/abstracted tracking (ETA, proximity zone, stage-based updates like 'Picked up', 'Nearby', 'Arriving') — requires BOTH teams to move off their 'non-negotiable' positions","D: Make it driver-opt-in with incentive — couriers who consent to location sharing get a per-delivery bonus, preserving driver agency while enabling the feature"],"recommendation":"Option C or D. The customer's actual need is delivery predictability, not surveillance. An ETA-based or proximity-zone approach ('Your courier is 5 minutes away') likely satisfies the customer job-to-be-done without creating the surveillance dynamic that drives driver churn. However, I am NOT authorized to resolve this — this requires executive arbitration with Legal/Compliance input. Do not build anything until this is resolved."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal/Compliance has not been consulted on sharing courier GPS data with customers. In GDPR/CCPA jurisdictions, courier location is personal data. Sharing exact coordinates with third parties (customers) requires lawful basis, potentially explicit courier consent, data minimization assessment, and DPIA. This could make Option A legally impermissible regardless of business preference.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/Compliance review before any feature design","Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment","Review courier contracts for location-sharing consent clauses"],"recommendation":"Block all feature work until Legal/Compliance provides guidance. If exact GPS sharing is unlawful in operating jurisdictions, this resolves the contradiction automatically."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot produce valid acceptance criteria from contradictory requirements","business_rule_broken":"Requirements from Customer Success and Operations are mutually exclusive — implementing either one violates the other team's stated non-negotiable constraint"},{"violation":"Legal/Compliance review absent for personal data sharing feature","business_rule_broken":"Any feature sharing worker location data with third parties requires legal review before specification, not after implementation"}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency, Operations demands no location sharing. Both claim their requirement is non-negotiable. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_cont
